varieties.name: Thai White
varieties.traits: Large (200-400g), round to oval, green skin, minimal seeds
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, crisp, crunchy texture like apple, mildly sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for humid subtropical climates subtropical climate. Highly humidity-resistant, tolerates humid subtropical climates summer heat well. Popular in Queensland nurseries. Can be eaten firm or soft.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Daleys Fruit, Bulleen Art Garden, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Hawaiian Pink Supreme
varieties.traits: Large (180-250g), yellow skin when ripe, oval to round
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep pink, juicy, sweet with tropical fruit aroma
varieties.growing_notes: Performs excellently in humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Very hardy and adaptable to humidity. Suitable for both fresh eating and juicing. Available from specialist Queensland nurseries.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Bulleen Art Garden

varieties.name: Mexican Cream
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy white, thick, very sweet, aromatic with pineapple-passionfruit notes

varieties.name: China Pear
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, crisp, sweet with good flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Well-adapted to Queensland subtropical conditions. Good disease resistance. Available from Australian nurseries, can be grown in containers.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit, Queensland fruit tree nurseries

varieties.name: Pink Supreme
varieties.traits: Medium to large, round, yellow skin
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically bred for Australian subtropical conditions including humid subtropical climates. Excellent humidity tolerance and heat resistance. Good for fresh eating.
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries, Rare Fruit Club WA

varieties.name: Strawberry Guava - Red (Psidium cattleianum)
varieties.traits: Small (2-3cm), dark red to purple skin, compact tree (2-3m)
varieties.yield_notes: Very high for size, prolific over 2-3 month period (April-June)

varieties.name: Strawberry Guava - Yellow (Psidium cattleianum var. lucidum)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy white, very sweet, aromatic, mild
varieties.yield_notes: Very high, more prolific than red variety

varieties.name: Brazilian Guava (Psidium guineense)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow with white acidic pulp, guava-strawberry flavor, sweet-sour
varieties.yield_notes: Good, can be pruned to size

varieties.name: Ruby X Supreme
varieties.traits: Pear-shaped, yellow skin, thick skin (fruit fly resistant)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick pink flesh, sweet and delicious

varieties.name: Red Malaysian / Thai Maroon
varieties.traits: Deep multi-colored leaves, incredible peeling bark, ornamental
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pinkish pulp, sweet and tart

varieties.name: Indian Guava
varieties.traits: Medium, yellow skin, can grow to 4m (prune to size)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White firm flesh with seeds, juicy, sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Can be grown in pots and containers. Frost tender but handles summer heat and humidity well.

varieties.name: Beaumont
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink, sweet with mild sour taste, passion fruit-watermelon-mint aroma
varieties.yield_notes: Very high, heavy producer, fall/winter harvest

varieties.name: Giant Vietnamese / Bangkok
varieties.traits: Very large (largest of all guava varieties)
varieties.growing_notes: Considered a superfruit, used for commercial guava juice worldwide. Suitable for humid subtropical climates tropical to subtropical conditions. Handles heat and humidity excellently.

varieties.name: Yellow Sweet
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, juicy, yellow or pink-blush flesh

varieties.name: Feijoa / Pineapple Guava (Acca sellowiana)
varieties.origin: Southern Brazil, eastern Paraguay, Uruguay, northern Argentina, Colombia - heritage variety
varieties.traits: Egg-sized, green skin, evergreen tree, drought-tolerant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Highly aromatic, combination of pineapple-apple-mint flavor

varieties.name: Allahabad Safeda
varieties.origin: Prayagraj (Allahabad), Uttar Pradesh, India - heritage variety from 1800s, most famous Indian guava
varieties.traits: Round, 180-200g, smooth skin, greenish-yellow when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, firm, soft skin, pleasantly sweet, few seeds
varieties.yield_notes: High, 20-25 tons per hectare, very stable

varieties.name: Lucknow 49 (L-49 / Sardar)
varieties.origin: Selection from open-pollinated Allahabad Safeda seedlings, Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh - heritage variety
varieties.traits: Large, round, 180-200g, greenish-yellow with rough surface, semi-tall spreading tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Milky white, very sweet, thick shell, few soft seeds

varieties.name: Allahabad Surkha 🏆
varieties.origin: Chance seedling from Abbubakarpur village, Allahabad district, India - GI tag 2008
varieties.traits: Apple-red exterior, uniform pink color, round
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep pink pulp, succulent, sweet, strongly flavored

varieties.name: Lalit
varieties.origin: Central Institute for Subtropical Horticulture, Lucknow - hybrid variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, pinkish, good mix of sweet and sour, TSS vitamin C 250mg/100g
varieties.yield_notes: Very high, 100-125kg per plant annually (highest in group)
varieties.growing_notes: Modern hybrid excellent for tropical monsoon climates monsoon climate. Suitable for both table and processing. Better jam flavor and appearance. Increasingly popular in Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh.

varieties.name: Arka Mridula
varieties.origin: ICAR-Indian Institute of Horticultural Research, hybrid
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, firm, thick outer rind, good flavor, soft seeds, TSS 10-12°B, vitamin C 190-198mg/100g

varieties.name: Arka Kiran
varieties.flesh_fruit: Red pulp, low oxalate content (28.3mg/100g), medium lycopene (5.0mg/100g), medium seed hardiness (9.0 kg/cm²)
varieties.growing_notes: Modern hybrid suitable for tropical monsoon climates climate. Rich in ascorbic acid. Developed for both table and processing uses. Adapts well to tropical monsoon conditions.
varieties.availability: IIHR, agricultural institutes, tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Shweta (Swetha)
varieties.origin: Central Institute for Subtropical Horticulture, Lucknow
varieties.traits: Medium size, spherical, velvety white skin with red spots
varieties.flesh_fruit: Nearly white, sweet taste, pleasing flavor when ripe, few seeds

varieties.name: Chittidar
varieties.origin: India, heritage variety, similar to Safeda
varieties.traits: Many pinkish-red dots size of pinhead on skin, 3.5-4.5m height

varieties.name: Pant Prabhat
varieties.origin: GB Pant University of Agriculture, hybrid
varieties.growing_notes: Modern hybrid suitable for tropical monsoon climates climate. Developed for commercial cultivation. Well-adapted to monsoon conditions.
varieties.availability: Agricultural universities, tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Safed Jam
varieties.growing_notes: Hybrid developed specifically for processing/jam making. Suitable for tropical monsoon climates monsoon climate. Higher pectin content ideal for preserves.

varieties.name: Kohir Safeda 🧬
varieties.origin: Hybrid between Allahabad Safeda and Kohir (local Karnataka collection)
varieties.traits: Similar to Allahabad Safeda, bigger fruit, good quality
varieties.growing_notes: Indigenous Karnataka hybrid using local collection. Suitable for tropical monsoon climates climate. Combines best traits of Allahabad Safeda with local adaptation.

varieties.name: Arka Amulya
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIHR, hybrid (cross between Kamsari and Purple Local)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep pink, high ascorbic acid and lycopene content, medium soft seeds, TSS 11°Brix
varieties.growing_notes: Modern hybrid excellent for tropical monsoon climates climate. High nutritional value. Suitable for both table and processing.
varieties.availability: IIHR, tropical monsoon climates agricultural nurseries

varieties.name: VNR-Bihi
varieties.origin: VNR Nursery, Chhattisgarh, India - modern development (2010s)

varieties.name: Anakapalli 🏆
varieties.origin: Anakapalli, Andhra Pradesh - indigenous regional variety
varieties.growing_notes: Indigenous heirloom from Andhra Pradesh. Well-suited to South India tropical climate including tropical monsoon climates. Regional cultural significance.

varieties.name: Deshi Karnataka 🏆
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink, natural sweetness, higher pulp content

varieties.name: Rudra Red
varieties.traits: Red color variety, can grow in any tropical areas

varieties.name: Red Seedless
varieties.traits: Pear-shaped, green rind, becomes maroon when ripe

varieties.name: Rica
varieties.growing_notes: Brazilian cultivar suitable for tropical climates including tropical monsoon climates monsoon conditions.

varieties.name: Le Dai Loan
varieties.growing_notes: Popular Vietnamese cultivar. Suitable for tropical monsoon climates monsoon climate with high humidity adaptation.
